title: maerua bussei
intro: Maerua bussei is a plant species native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, commonly known as the "Gamboge bush" due to its yellow sap, which is similar to the resin of the Gamboge tree. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to help treat various health issues. The health benefits of Maerua bussei include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antimicrobial agent, among others. In traditional herbal preparations, the sap of the Maerua bussei plant is often extracted and used in various forms, such as capsules, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties.
